recalls.Description:
JJGoo has issued a recall for its LED balloon lights due to safety concerns. These submersible lights, sold in packs of 100, feature color-changing LEDs and contain 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries. Each light is approximately 0.6 inches in diameter and is labeled with "MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" on the packaging. The design allows easy access to the batteries, posing a significant ingestion risk to children.

The recalled lights do not meet the safety requirements outlined by Reese's Law, which mandates specific warnings for products containing button cell batteries. Swallowing these batteries can lead to severe injuries, including internal chemical burns and even death. It is crucial for consumers to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Due to Serious Injury Risk from Battery Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact JJGoo via email at JJGooLEDBalloonLightsrecall@outlook.com.

"MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" is printed on a label on the product packaging of the recalled JJGoo LED balloon lights

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yimu Technology Co., Ltd. dba JJGoo, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights, keep them away from children, and dispose of batteries properly; contact JJGoo for a full refund.

recalls.Description: The Isla Rae brand magnetic wireless chargers have been recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. These chargers, which attach magnetically to the back of compatible phones, were sold in white, pink, and purple colors. The model number 'RM5PBM' is located on the side of the charger, beneath the label '5000 mAh 3.7V.' It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire risks, as they cannot be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.

recalls.Description: INIU is recalling its 10,000mAh portable power banks, specifically model BI-B41, due to potential fire and burn hazards. recalls.Description: KTEBO has issued a recall for its Writing Tablet Toys due to a serious safety hazard. These toys, sold in pairs and available in four color combinations (pink/blue, green/yellow, orange/blue, purple/red), come in two sizes: 8.5 inches and 10 inches, each accompanied by a stylus. The toys fail to meet safety standards due to a faulty battery compartment screw, which can pose a risk of battery ingestion. Ingested button cell batteries can lead to severe injuries, internal burns, or even death.

The recall now includes additional ESR HaloLock wireless power banks, specifically model 2G505, alongside previously recalled models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B. These pocket-sized power banks feature the brand name "ESR" on the back and model numbers on the right side. Model 2G505 is available in black and white, while the other models come in various colors including dark blue, light blue, gray, white, and black. recalls.Description: Zyntony has announced a recall of Kogalla power banks, specifically models BP125, BatPak 2F, and BatPak 3F. These rechargeable USB power banks, equipped with lithium-ion batteries of 6,700mAh, 13,400mAh, or 20,100mAh capacities, were sold individually and as part of Kogalla trail light packages. The Kogalla logo is visible on the front, while the model number can be found on the back. Notably, the BP125 model features a black USB-A jack, whereas the BatPak 2F and 3F models have orange USB-A jacks. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled lithium-ion batteries in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire risk. Instead, consumers should check with their local hazardous waste collection centers for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Zyntony Issues Recall for Kogalla Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Kogalla via email at bpreplace@kogalla.com, or visit https://rtn.kogalla.com/pages/kogalla-recalls-batpak1-batpak-2f-batpak-3f or https://kogalla.com/ and click on “Recall Information” for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - front
Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - back

Injuries.Name: Zyntony has received two reports of lithium-ion battery overheating and catching fire, even when not in use, resulting in one minor burn injury and property damage, totaling about $3,300.

Retailers.Name: Available on Kogalla.com from June 2024 to January 2025 for the BP125 and BatPak 2F models, as well as the trail lights sold with them; and from August 2024 to January 2025 for the BatPak 3F model and its associated trail lights. Prices ranged from $45 to $90 for the power banks and from $180 to $230 for the trail lights with power banks.

Importers.Name: Zyntony Inc. of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition of lithium-ion batteries, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled power banks and contact Kogalla for a free replacement, including shipping. An online form at https://rtn.kogalla.com/products/request-batpak-replacement must be completed. Proper disposal of the recalled power banks is also required per local regulations.

recalls.Description: The Arizer Solo II, a portable vaporizer powered by a lithium-ion battery, is being recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. This device features a sturdy anodized aluminum body and comes in Carbon Black and Mystic Blue. It has a digital display for temperature settings and a stainless steel heating chamber. Only units with serial numbers beginning with 'M2' are affected, which can be found etched on the bottom of the device. The product packaging includes UPC 628078800836.

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the Arizer Solo II immediately and reach out to 7111495 Canada at 888-291-0521 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@arizer.com or visit https://arizer.com/solo-ii-voluntary-recall-submission-form/ or www.arizer.ca and click on 'Product Recalls' for further details.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack, a wireless portable charger featuring rechargeable lithium-ion batteries. These power banks are black, measuring approximately three inches in length and two inches in width, with the Quad Lock logo on the front and the model number on the back. It is crucial to handle recalled lithium-ion batteries properly, as they can pose safety risks. Dispose of them according to local regulations and avoid throwing them in the trash or placing them in standard battery recycling bins.
recalls.Title: Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack Recall Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Quad Lock at 833-362-9855 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email recall@quadlock.com.au. More information is available at quadlockcase.net/recall or by visiting www.quadlockcase.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,700 (In addition, about 1,580 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack (front)
Recalled Quad Lock MAG Battery Pack (rear)

Injuries.Name: Quad Lock has received 39 reports of incidents in the United States, including three reports of fire and 36 reports of smoking, melting, cracking or swelling, resulting in two reports of property damage. Quad Lock has received 170 reports of incidents internationally, including four reports of fire and 166 reports of smoking, melting, cracking or swelling, resulting in one report of property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.quadlockcase.com, www.amazon.com, and various third-party sellers from April 2023 through September 2024, priced between $60 and $80.

Importers.Name: Annex Products Pty Ltd., dba Quad Lock, of Australia

Hazards.Name: Overheating battery pack posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled battery packs, remove them from the charger, and contact Quad Lock for a full refund or store credit. Dispose of lithium-ion batteries per local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Quad Lock recalls MAG Battery Packs due to fire hazards. Learn how to return your charger for a refund and safe disposal methods.

recalls.Description: Humane has issued a recall for the Charge Case Accessory designed for the Ai Pin, an advanced AI-powered wearable device. This accessory features a lithium polymer rechargeable battery that can overheat, creating a potential fire hazard. The Charge Case is chrome-colored with the Humane logo in gray and measures approximately 2 ¾ x 1 ½ x 2 ¼ inches. It is important to note that this recall pertains solely to the Charge Case Accessory and does not affect any other Humane products.
recalls.Title: Humane Recalls Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin Due to Fire Risk from Lithium Battery
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Humane at 844-481-1900 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@humane.com, or visit https://humane.com/ccrecall or https://humane.com/aipin and select "Charge Case Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Humane Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin

Images.Caption: Recalled Humane Inc Ai Pin Charge Case
Recalled Humane Inc Ai Pin Charge Case

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a charge case overheating and melting during charging. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase at humane.com from November 2023 through May 2024. The Charge Case Accessory was sold separately for about $150 and as part of the Humane Ai Pin Complete System for between $700 and $800.

Importers.Name: Humane Inc., of San Francisco, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium battery posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the Charge Case Accessory immediately and contact Humane for a full refund or a free replacement. Refunds will be $149 for the accessory purchased alone and $129 for those who bought it as part of the Ai Pin Complete System. A replacement will be available in approximately 3 to 6 months. Please dispose of the accessory according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Humane recalls Charge Case Accessory for Ai Pin due to fire hazard from overheating lithium battery. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Anker is recalling specific models of its Power Banks, identified as A1642, A1647, and A1652. These portable chargers are designed for smartphones, tablets, and laptops, with maximum outputs of 12W, 15W, and 20W respectively. Each power bank features the Anker logo on the front, while the model numbers can be found on the back. Consumers should be aware of potential fire and burn hazards associated with these devices.
recalls.Title: Recall of Anker Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For more details, check https://www.anker.com/product-recalls or visit www.anker.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Retailers.Name: Best Buy

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled power banks and contact Anker Innovations to obtain a free replacement. Visit https://www.anker.com/product-recalls to register for the recall and ensure proper disposal of batteries per local regulations.

recalls.Description: The recall affects Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets that include button cell batteries. Each set contains 250 glow sticks, 12 foam glow sticks, 12 LED glasses, and 12 bunny ear headbands in various colors. The presence of easily accessible button cell batteries poses a significant ingestion hazard to children. If swallowed, these batteries can lead to serious injuries, internal burns, or even death.
recalls.Title: Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets Recalled Due to Ingestion Risk; Battery Safety Violation; Sold Exclusively on Amazon by OleOleToy
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, reach out to OleOleToy via email at degong99@yeah.net or visit their Amazon.com Seller Profile: OleOleToy Direct-High Quality.

Importers.Name: Yiwu Shanshi Trading Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries in Bunny Ears Headbands, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the Bunny Ears Headbands, remove them from children, cut off the battery compartment, and contact OleOleToy for a full refund. recalls.Description:
HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations, which are high-capacity lithium-ion battery power banks, are being recalled due to serious fire and burn hazards. These power stations feature three USB-A charge ports, a USB-C fast charge port, three AC outputs, two DC outputs, a DC socket, an LCD display, a built-in flood light, and a collapsible handle. The recall includes various models identified by their colors and specific SKU numbers.

Affected models include Stone Gray, Carbon, Desert Sand, Midnight Blue, and Woodland Green, all under model number PS-1000. The SKU and UPC codes for these products can be found on the bottom of the units. Notably, units sold by QVC were listed under item number E235474.
recalls.Title: HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks; One Fatal Incident Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please call the HALO 1000 recall hotline at 888-345-0481 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email halo5528@sedgwick.com. More information is available online at https://halo1000recall.expertinquiry.com/.

Products.Name: HALO 1000 Portable Power Station - Stone Gray

Images.Caption: Recalled HALO 1000 Portable Power Station
Recalled HALO 1000 Portable Power Station

Injuries.Name: A 79-year-old man in Bradenton, Florida died from smoke inhalation from a fire involving the recalled portable power station in his home in June 2022. In addition to the death, the firms have collectively received four reports of fires involving the HALO 1000 Portable Power Station, including two reports of smoke inhalation, one of which included burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen DBK Electronics Co. Ltd.,of China

Retailers.Name: Available from ZAGG, ACG, and QVC, approximately 5,740 units were sold between October 2021 and March 2022, priced between $1,000 and $1,300.

Importers.Name: ZAGG Inc. (ZAGG), of Midvale, Utah

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power stations and contact the HALO Recall hotline for a free replacement

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations due to fire hazards. Contact for free replacements.

recalls.Description: IKEA has announced a recall of two models of VARMFRONT Power Banks, specifically model numbers E2037 and E2038. Both models are blue in color, with model E2037 featuring one USB-A port and one USB-C port, measuring approximately four inches long and two inches wide. It includes date codes (YYWW) 2318, 2319, or 2322. Model E2038, on the other hand, has two USB-A ports and one USB-C port, is about four inches long and three inches wide, and has date codes 2313, 2316, 2318, or 2319. The model number appears on the top of the back side, while the date code is printed on the bottom of the power banks.
recalls.Title: IKEA Recalls VARMFRONT Power Banks Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach IKEA at 888-966-4532 from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information is available online at https://www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls or by visiting https://www.ikea.com/us/en and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Baseus has initiated a recall of its magnetic wireless charging power banks, specifically models PPCXM06 and PPCXW06. These power banks, available in white, black, light blue, and light pink, feature a magnetic side designed for attachment to compatible mobile phones. The model number is located on the magnetic side, while '20W' is marked on the non-magnetic side. Model PPCXM06 measures approximately 4 in. x 2-½ in. x 0.64 in., and PPCXW06 measures about 4 in. x 2-½ in. x ½ in. Only the specified models are affected, with some units also listed on Amazon under the model number PPXCW06.
recalls.Title: Recall of Baseus Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Banks Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Baseus at 855-215-5824 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email care@baseus.com, or visit https://baseuspowerbankrecall.expertinquiry.com. More details can also be found at www.baseus.com under 'Product Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Baseus and Amazon will notify all known purchasers. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

recalls.Description: myCharge has issued a recall for its POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh portable chargers, identified by model numbers AO10FK-A, AO10FK-B, and AO10FK-C. These chargers, which are black and feature two built-in cables for charging devices along with retractable wall prongs, are marked with the brand name "myCharge" on the front. The model number can be found on the back, and the date code indicating the manufacturing period is located on the wall prong's outer surface. The affected date codes end with 21 or 22, or are among the following codes ending in 23: 0123, 0923, 1523, or 1723.
recalls.Title: myCharge Recalls POWER HUB All-In-One Portable Chargers Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled chargers immediately and contact myCharge at 888-251-2026 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional assistance can be obtained via email at compliance@mycharge.com or by visiting https://mycharge.com/pages/power-hub-all-in-one-recall or www.myCharge.com and selecting "RECALL INFORMATION" at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Yoto Ltd. has issued a recall for the Yoto Mini, a portable speaker designed for children aged three to twelve. This device, which operates using Yoto cards, plays audiobooks, music, radio, and podcasts. The Yoto Mini features a pale gray body with orange controls and a pixel display. The model name Yoto Mini, along with SKU PRPLXX00860, can be found on the bottom of the speaker. The recalled units were sold with a charging cable but did not include an adaptor or charger.
recalls.Title: Yoto Mini Speakers for Kids Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yoto Ltd. at 844-370-0426 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, via email at minihelp@yotoplay.com, or visit https://yotoplay.com/recall. You can also find more information by going to https://us.yotoplay.com/ and clicking on 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description: Yamaha is recalling its PA-10 AC Power Adaptors, which have date codes from 2126 to 2319. The power adapter, marked with 'Yamaha' on the front, may develop cracks between its upper and lower cases, potentially exposing electrical wiring and creating serious electrical shock and electrocution hazards. This recall specifically affects the PA-10 AC Power Adapter, which was included with several Yamaha analog mixing console models and sold separately as a replacement part.
recalls.Title: Yamaha PA-10 AC Power Adaptor Recall Due to Shock and Electrocution Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Yamaha toll-free at 844-703-5446 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email pa10recall@yamaha.com. Additional information is available online at www.yamaha.com/us/support/pa10recall/ or by visiting www.usa.yamaha.com and selecting 'Product Safety Information' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Vernier Science Education has issued a recall for the Go Direct Charge Station, which is designed to charge the Go Direct line of wireless sensors with rechargeable batteries. These sensors are commonly utilized in educational environments for various science experiments, including data collection on temperature, oxygen levels, carbon dioxide levels, and pH values. The Go Direct Charge Station can simultaneously charge up to 16 sensors using USB and wand-style ports. It is identifiable by the order code GDX-CRG and model number GDXCRG2617R1, located on a label at the bottom of the unit. This product may have been sold individually or as part of a Teacher Pack with order codes GDX-TMP-TP or GDX-PH-TP.
recalls.Title: Vernier Science Education Recalls Go Direct Charge Stations Due to Overheating Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the Go Direct Charge Station immediately and reach out to Vernier Science Education at 888-837-6437, available from 6:45 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Thursday, and from 6:45 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT on Friday. For further assistance, email support@vernier.com or visit www.vernier.com/gdx-crg-recall. More information can also be found by clicking the orange banner on the Vernier homepage regarding the safety recall.
